hero.title: Join Mōchirīī
hero.intro: Mōchirīī is a guild home for players who enjoy good company, clear roles & pretty greetings. Discord is the main door; the website helps you keep your account, events & profile in step.

steps.title: The Joining Path
intro: These steps carry you from hello to first run. Discord handles the live welcome; the website keeps the guides, profile tools & event trail easy to find.

items.title: Enter the Discord hall
items.description: Join Discord, follow the server rules and onboarding prompts, then say you are here for the guild. If a prompt or channel feels confusing, ask a mod before guessing.

items.title: Verify and share your IGN
items.description: Complete verification, then share your in-game name or UID and time zone. This helps leaders match you with the right roles, channels and event windows.

items.title: Read the Codex and Ranks
items.description: Skim the Codex and Ranks pages so you know the guild tone, etiquette, leadership path and support flow before you settle in.

items.title: Set up your website account
items.description: After Discord verification, sign in on the website to check your member status, profile basics and gallery access. Keep Discord as the source for live roles.

items.title: Join your first event
items.description: Pick an upcoming event, RSVP if planning is needed and ask questions early. Showing up prepared helps the run start well and gives people an easy reason to remember you.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
body: Join Discord first, then use the website to read, verify and keep track of your next step.
Questions are welcome. A clear hello is enough to begin.

checklist.title: New Cupcake Checklist
checklist.intro: A quick way to step in with the right roles, context and first plan.

items.title: Join the Discord
items.text: Use Discord for live announcements, event planning and welcome messages.
items.label: Join Discord

items.title: Accept the server rules
items.text: Complete Discord's rules and onboarding prompts before asking for roles. They keep the first steps clear and help protect the server.

items.title: Complete verification
items.text: Verification opens the right channels once your role is set. If it stalls, ask a mod for help instead of repeating random steps.

items.title: Know your in-game name or UID
items.text: Let us know your IGN so we can find you quickly & welcome you by name.

items.title: Read the Codex
items.text: Review the guild's conduct, values & expectations before you settle in.
items.label: Read the Codex

items.title: Set up your profile
items.text: After you verify, open Account to check member status and start your public profile only when you are ready.
items.label: Open Account

items.title: Choose a first event
items.text: Events are announced as scheduled. Join when you can, speak up when life gets busy and keep the atmosphere friendly.
items.label: View Events

items.title: Ask where to go next
items.text: A simple greeting or help request gives leaders a clear place to guide you. Small welcomes become the start of shared runs.

culture.title: Expectations & Culture
intro: Mōchirīī stays kind through courtesy, effort & clear communication. Skill grows over time; care can show up in the first message.

cards.title: Respect & kindness
cards.description: Keep chat welcoming, assume good intent & treat every member as someone you may share the road with for a long time.

cards.title: Activity rhythm
cards.description: Participation stays flexible. Presence, RSVPs for planned runs & notice during breaks matter more than marathon sessions.

cards.title: Helping new members
cards.description: Questions belong here. Veterans share tips, builds & mechanics with patience. Leaders step in when guidance helps.

cards.title: Events & updates
cards.description: Events & announcements live in Discord as well as on the website, with pinned notes for clarity. RSVP early when asked & follow posted requirements.

notes.title: Notes
body: Voice chat is optional unless an event post says otherwise. Discord is the clearest place for rules, roles, announcements and coordination.
Need a break? Tell a leader before a longer Cupcake nap so roles & expectations remain for you.
If you feel lost, tag a leader or ask in the welcome/help channel. Getting unstuck keeps the game fun.
